scaled_oracle_cases.description: Combined scaled test covering mixed chromosome prefixes, varied annotation strings, and irregular whitespace.
scaled_oracle_cases.semantic_intent: Ensure the function handles mixed chromosome prefixes (with/without 'chr', 'chrom'), varied annotation strings (including '.' and 'NA'), and irregular whitespace (leading/trailing spaces, multiple spaces, tabs).
scaled_oracle_cases.target_constraint: Chromosome names are taken as-is. Missing annotations (no second token) become '-'. Whitespace is handled by strip() and split().
scaled_oracle_cases.expected_failure_mode: A fragile solution that expects 'chr' prefix, assumes specific annotations, or uses fixed split delimiter will fail.
scaled_oracle_cases.setup_code:
with open('scaled_input.txt', 'w') as f:
    f.write('  chr1:100-200   geneA  \n')
    f.write('chrX:300-400\tNA\n')
    f.write('chrom2:500-600 .\n')
    f.write('1:700-800\n')
    f.write('chr3:900-1000 A>T\n')
    f.write(' chr4:1100-1200 gene_symbol \n')
covered_requirements: Scaled executable cases must include a test file with various chromosome prefixes and verify correct parsing.
Scaled executable cases must include a test file with diverse annotation values and verify correct mapping.
Scaled executable cases must test whitespace variations and verify correct parsing.

scaled_oracle_cases.description: Empty input file should return an empty DataFrame with correct columns.
scaled_oracle_cases.semantic_intent: Verify that an empty file yields an empty DataFrame with columns ['chromosome', 'start', 'end', 'gene'].
scaled_oracle_cases.target_constraint: Return DataFrame with zero rows and the specified columns.
scaled_oracle_cases.expected_failure_mode: A solution that assumes at least one line will raise an exception or return a malformed DataFrame.
covered_requirements: Scaled executable cases must test empty input and verify DataFrame structure.
